design_philosophy.core_loop: Grid-based tactical combat with squad composition (4 characters per squad leader), dragon evolution through item feeding, and turn-based engagement. Players cycle through battles across floating lagoon continents, managing dragon loyalty and growth.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing squad composition, dragon evolution state, and tactical positioning on grid-based battlefields. Dragons act autonomously per player guidelines (Go/Wait), creating unpredictability.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a dragon squad across a war-torn sky kingdom, breeding and evolving dragons into unique forms while leading rebellion against occupying empire.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Dragons are not party members but autonomous AI-driven creatures with independent will—designer Ono explicitly rejected direct player control. Dragons evolve through feeding items (weapons/armor/accessories) and loyalty mechanics where disliked food weakens loyalty but increases power, creating strategic tension. This design philosophy (dragons as pets, not tools) fundamentally shapes how players approach composition and strategy.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through squad leader selection (6 options), dragon evolution choices, and item feeding decisions. Battle system designer Takatsuga Nakazawa described as 'brutal' with trap placements; system designed to be 'challenging yet balanced' but with intentional difficulty spikes.

progression.upgrade_system: Dragon evolution system: feed dragons weapons, armor, accessories, and items to transform them into different types. Loyalty system affects dragon behavior—likes increase loyalty (better performance), dislikes decrease loyalty but increase power (risk/reward). Character equipment and item acquisition through battle progression.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear scenario progression across continents of Orelus. Six different squadron leaders available for selection, each with their own dragon.
replayability_hooks: Six different squadron leaders with distinct dragons to experience alternate playthroughs
Dragon evolution paths dependent on feeding choices (different evolutionary outcomes)
Loyalty/power tradeoff in dragon development allows multiple optimization strategies

world_building_techniques: Setting inspired by floating lagoon concept (Izu Peninsula geography)
Silent protagonist (Byuu) allows player projection into narrative of rebellion
Plot features complex relationships and betrayals influenced by writer Motomu Toriyama's personal experiences
Kingdom invasion and occupation narrative with campaign arc structure

faction_indicators: Kingdom of Kahna (player faction) vs Granbelos Empire (antagonist)
Initial defeat and occupation creates resistance narrative
danger_signals: Empire invasion and occupation of home territory
Continued campaign against overwhelming enemy power
environmental_storytelling: Floating lagoon continents in sky (world geography reflects setting inspiration)
War-torn locations across Orelus reflecting conflict progression
